flags.description: Master toggle. Off = every write path in the age-group module returns 503. Emergency-only — flips down when the identity-service must freeze AgeGroup mutations (e.g. mid-migration or during a compliance-motivated freeze).
Off = ReconcileAgeGroupDefaultInvariantJob no-ops. Used during scheduled migrations that intentionally leave tenants in a temporary invariant-violating state (e.g. a bulk catalog reshuffle).

Independent of `age_group.reconciliation.auto_heal` config — when this flag is off, no auto-heal happens even if the config is on. Two layers of guard because auto-heal changes eligibility-attribution behaviour + must never surprise ops.
Off = PurgeArchivedAgeGroupsJob no-ops. Archived AgeGroups sit past their 1095-day retention window. Rare — used during legal holds when age-group evidence must be preserved.

Off = PATCH /{age_group} refuses any change to min_age_inclusive OR max_age_inclusive with 503. Used during safeguarding-audit windows when bounds must remain frozen while investigators reconstruct historical eligibility.
Off = PATCH /{age_group} refuses any change to cutoff_date_kind / cutoff_month / cutoff_day with 503. Same use case as bounds_edit_enabled.
Off = POST /{age_group}/set-default returns 503. Used during scheduled tier-pricing recomputation runs when the default must not flip mid-batch.
Off = GET /api/v1/age-groups/resolve returns 503. Used when a resolver bug is producing wrong answers — the read path is frozen while a fix is deployed.

Off = SeedDefaultAgeGroupsForTenantJob runs synchronously inside the tenancy provisioning transaction. Slower but safer (tenant is never briefly catalogless). On is the default because tenant onboarding must feel instant.
